A very bad experience with Norauto Marbella. I took my car in this morning with a prior appointment to have a simple punctured tire repaired, caused by a nail. They assured me it would be ready by 4:00 PM. After 5:15 PM, having received no calls or information, I had to go to the store in person. To my surprise, I found the car still hadn't been repaired, and instead of fixing the puncture, they gave me an estimate of over €200 to replace both tires. Given this situation, I filed a complaint and decided to take the car to another garage. There, they fixed the puncture in just 10 minutes for only €15. I find the lack of professionalism, the poor time management, and the attempt to sell an unnecessary repair when the solution was simple and inexpensive unacceptable. I will not be returning, and I certainly do not recommend them. I've given them one star because it won't let me give zero.

To begin with, they treat cars very poorly at the garage and don't pay attention to what you're asking for or explaining about your car's problem. You have to be very careful with the bill and what they add to it without your knowledge. For example, the "EcoRevision diesel" option includes a diesel level 1 check and a diesel level 2 "eco check," totaling almost €50 for no reason. Because that's so that if you need to top up the fluids during the year... they charge you upfront and with VAT! Which I find unacceptable. I don't understand why they charge you upfront. If I need to top up, I'll go and they'll charge me when I go to fill it up, but it doesn't make sense to charge you and then sneak it in on the oil change bill.
And I should also mention that everyone is very friendly, but they don't really pay much attention to your car or what you're asking for.

And the manager or boss or person in charge at Norauto Marbella is a surly, very rude, and extremely unpleasant man. I've been a customer for 27 years, but from now on, I'll be done with it.

All they care about is making money.
When you complain about a botched repair and a defective item, all they say is, "I can't do anything." Neither I nor anyone else who knows how they operate will ever go back.

I walked around the shopping center for an hour waiting for this appointment. When I returned to the salon, I once again explained what I wanted. At that point I was told that they had to wash my hair, and that the price would therefore be 36 euros.

I confronted them and pointed out that they had already seen what my hair looked like when they gave me the price of 21 euros, but they refused to meet me halfway in any way — even when the hairdresser admitted that she had given me the wrong price.

The hairdresser who quoted 21 euros should have told me at that time that the hair would need to be washed. Even after acknowledging their mistake, they refused to make any compromise. I even suggested meeting halfway at 30 euros since they had given incorrect information, but that was completely unacceptable to them.

I decided to leave and instead found another, more professional hairdresser.

A few years ago, I decided to get my driver's license at this driving school, and my experience was completely negative.

Sergio, who is both the theory and practical instructor and the owner along with his wife (who manages administration), constantly told me the exam would be on a certain date, but then he would cancel or postpone it time and time again. It felt like they were trying to drag out my training so I would keep paying for more practical lessons.

Furthermore, they assured me there were examiner strikes and other problems preventing me from taking the exam, while I almost always had to do the practical lessons alone because "the other students weren't showing up." This meant paying for more practice time and a much higher expense for me.

For over a year, I was trapped in this situation, watching as practically all the students failed and everything dragged on pointlessly. Finally, I decided to switch driving schools, and even then, Sergio kept calling and texting me demanding explanations.

I absolutely do not recommend this driving school. My experience was very frustrating, a huge waste of time, money, and patience.

I engaged this firm in July 2025 for legal and accounting support for my Spanish company, clearly explaining from the outset that the shareholder is a Lithuanian parent company and that accounting output would be required for audit and consolidation purposes.

All invoices were paid promptly, including additional requests made during the engagement.

Despite this, the deliverables provided consisted of Excel-based accounting information which, while potentially suitable for internal use, were not usable for audit or consolidation purposes as required and previously communicated.

No finalized written agreement clearly defining the scope of services was provided to me, despite repeated requests. This created a lack of clarity as to what was included in the engagement.

Regarding the 2025 Corporate Income Tax and Annual Accounts, the firm’s position changed multiple times. The work was first indicated to be outside scope, then offered under specific terms, and subsequently withdrawn after acceptance.

As a result, I was required to engage a new advisory firm to ensure compliance and completion of statutory obligations.

This review reflects my experience based on documented correspondence and the outcome of the engagement.

We recently purchased a property in Marbella through this agency.

The buyer's experience was quite stressful. After reserving the property, we felt pressured to sign the deposit contract quickly. Since we were using bank financing, we had to adhere to the bank's processing times. The deposit was nevertheless paid on schedule.

Once the contract was signed, our lawyer requested the necessary documents to prepare for the signing at the notary's office. Despite the time between signing the deposit and the final signing, some important documents were still missing as the appointment approached.

On the day of the signing at the notary's office, a crucial document was still missing about an hour before the signing, which could have jeopardized the sale. The situation was ultimately resolved urgently.

Furthermore, due to a last-minute document, part of the payment had to be made in cash on the day of signing at the notary's office, which made the situation particularly uncomfortable for us as foreign buyers.

We are currently documenting our property purchase journey in Spain to help other French people who wish to buy in Marbella. This experience will be one of the topics we will share and explain in more detail in our upcoming content.

The sale was ultimately completed, but we would have appreciated more advance preparation and a smoother organization of the necessary documents for the transaction.

I'm sharing my experience with this Malaga-based company that I found through a contact. We've had an air conditioning unit for several years now. Recently, in 2025, it stopped cooling. My wife called to ask about repairs. That same day, a technician called my wife and told us they would come to our home to see if it could be fixed and that the visit, which we had to pay for, would be deducted from the total price once it was installed. My wife told him it wasn't urgent, that we had other things to do that day and wouldn't be available. The technician said it wouldn't take long and they showed up at our house while we were having lunch. They asked us what was wrong, we told them it wasn't cooling, and they simply told us it couldn't be fixed and that we would receive a quote by email. They didn't inform us about alternatives, equipment, etc. They gave us a quote, but the equipment they're going to install isn't what we need. We want a unit that works with Wi-Fi, since our home is automated, and a brand compatible with the other unit we already have. As we told the woman, we're interested in installing an air conditioning unit, but we're not obligated to install the unit the company chooses without offering us alternatives, specifications, or prices. After that, it was agreed that we would contact them again in 2026, but we've been receiving emails for some time now indicating that we owe around €151. And to make matters worse, we just received one threatening to take us to court. Anyway, I could understand if a technician came to my house and did a survey or some work, I would pay them. But in this case, they did nothing except tell us we would receive a quote. They also didn't tell us that we had a deadline for replacing the unit and buying the new one. In our case, I would not recommend this company to family or friends and would look for another alternative.

I am very dissatisfied with the treatment I received from the employees, especially the cashier. I bought a pair of defective headphones a little over a month ago, and they refuse to exchange them directly, even though they themselves confirmed that the item is defective. They refuse to give me the option of choosing another product or a refund, as I purchased a faulty item that is clearly beyond repair (confirmed by them, and it is my right as a consumer to receive a refund). They tell me I have to wait an EXCESSIVE amount of time—6-8 weeks—to send it to the technical team. I don't want the same item because I haven't had a good experience. I have filed a complaint and will be taking it to the consumer protection agency.

I recently went to buy a book with my mother. I've always liked this store because of its wide variety and because it carries titles that aren't easily found elsewhere.

However, this time the experience was negative. When we went to pay, there were no employees at the register, only trainees who couldn't help us. They told us to go to customer service.

There we found a man who, judging by his position, we understood to be in charge of the technology department (he was bald). I asked him if he could ring us up or, failing that, let a colleague know. He refused both times, and he was also inattentive and uninterested in helping us.

Given this situation, I told him that if no one could help us, I would leave the book unpurchased. His response was that he wasn't going to do anything about it, which I found to be a completely inappropriate attitude towards customers.

Finally, upon returning to the trainee staff area, a bookstore manager appeared, attended to us politely, and apologized, demonstrating a professional attitude.

I believe that this type of behavior from some employees seriously damages the establishment's image and can lead to the loss of customers. It would be advisable to strengthen customer service training and reinforce company values ​​to prevent similar situations.

Thank you.

one_star_reviews.text: I took my motorcycle to the workshop under warranty with an appointment, traveling all the way from Malaga since appointments there take a month. After waiting a while, I was finally seen by an advisor named Massimo. If you get this advisor, it's best to wait for another one or go to a different workshop, because this one has absolutely no professionalism. The man seemed very helpful and friendly. He told me he would keep me informed about the whole process, the problem with the motorcycle, the repair time, and when I could pick it up. After four days in the workshop and many calls, he finally deigned to call me back, frustrated, to say there was nothing wrong with the motorcycle, that they were going out to test it again, and that he would call me back in the afternoon. But... nobody called back. I kept calling, nobody answered, nobody replied to WhatsApp messages. I don't understand this man's complete indifference.

You're a respected customer until the moment you buy. Personally, I've had enough of this brand. I own a 545 that I bought new. Today I went there to ask for a bodywork repair estimate. The first shocking question was, "Why do I need to know? I have insurance, they'll sort it out." I had to explain that my share is very high, and I wanted to know the price. They told me to make an appointment in ten days for the estimate and informed me that it would cost €50. As a matter of principle, no more BMWs.

A deplorable lack of communication from the MINI workshop. My 2024 Clubman (under warranty) is being repaired after an accident, and it's impossible to get any information. Neither the workshop responds to my messages, nor has mediation by MINI Spain's Customer Service been successful in getting them to contact me after 48 hours. As a customer of a new MINI, I feel completely ignored and disappointed with the service at Automotor Marbella. In fact, this is my sixth MINI, and I'm considering selling it because of all the stress this situation is causing me. It's truly appalling.

A completely disappointing experience with BMW Marbella and the Safamotor group (of Malaga) in general.

After an accident last November, my vehicle (a BMW X5) was taken to their facilities, where it remained for over two months in an outdoor area without anyone even inspecting the car or starting the repairs. Two months completely wasted, without explanation or solution.

Faced with this situation, I was forced to take the vehicle myself, along with my insurance company, to BMW Jerez so that they would at least begin processing it.

As a direct consequence of this initial lack of action, the repairs have been greatly delayed. To this day, they tell me that critical parts (axle and output shaft) are missing and could take up to 3 or 4 additional months to arrive.

The result is surreal: for an accident that only affected the rear (wheel, rim, and axle), I could be without a vehicle for up to 8 months.

The accident was not BMW's fault, but their subsequent handling of the matter has been absolutely disastrous. A level of service unbecoming of a brand like BMW.

My experience at this restaurant was negative, mainly due to the lack of organization and service management.
We waited in line for over 20 minutes with two young children. During that time, we saw several large tables become available, tables that could have easily accommodated us. However, instead of preparing them for the customers already waiting, they decided to seat several groups of two and three people who had arrived after us, even at tables that were still dirty and had been set up while they were already seated. This seemed inconsiderate and disrespectful, especially considering we had young children with us.
When I politely explained the situation to the person in charge of organizing the tables, I didn't receive an appropriate response. Far from showing understanding or apologizing, they simply justified it by saying they had to prepare tables for four people. I explained that those same tables could have been prepared for us, just as they had been for other customers who arrived later. At that point, they offered us a table. However, by then the way everything had been handled and their attitude made us feel uncomfortable, and we decided to leave. It wasn't just because it's an expensive place, but because the lack of organization and, above all, the staff's inadequate response made us feel uneasy.
I understand that these kinds of situations can be due to oversights or periods of high workload, and I have no problem accepting that. However, what I consider important is the attitude towards the customer: a simple apology or a minimal show of empathy would have completely changed our perception.

Assessment:
Called to site to carry out an independent engineer’s inspection and report on an existing air conditioning installation completed by another contractor approximately 8–12 months ago.
Findings:
* The refrigerant pipework located within the bathroom ceiling void is installed without insulation (“socks”). Bare copper pipe is exposed. This will cause condensation forming during operation, which can lead to water dripping, ceiling damage, and long-term moisture issues. Proper insulation is required.
* The installed system capacity is 8 kW. Based on the apartment size (approx. 100+ m²), this unit appears undersized for the cooling/heating demand. A system closer to 10 kW capacity would typically be recommended to correctly condition this space and avoid poor performance or excessive runtime.
* The system currently contains no refrigerant gas. This indicates a leak or previous loss of charge.
Possible causes include:
* Leak within  pipework
* Leak within indoor or outdoor unit coil
* Poor flare/connection joints from installation
* General installation defect
At present, the exact leak location cannot be confirmed without further testing.
Recommended next steps:
* Nitrogen pressure test and full leak detection to locate the faul

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to you regarding a dispute concerning a glazing order placed with your company.

During installation, my installer discovered that the delivered glass did not match the required dimensions. This error clearly resulted from incorrect measurements.

As a reminder, Mr. Eric Abella, representing you, introduced me to Mr. José Antonio Jimenez, who visited on November 24, 2025, to personally take the necessary measurements for the final quote. Following this visit, a new quote, significantly different from the first, was sent to me and I accepted it, which led to the order being placed.

Mr. Jimenez had indicated a delivery date of early January, which ultimately did not occur until mid-February. At the start of the installation, my installer immediately alerted me to the fact that the glazing dimensions did not correspond to the required measurements.

I then contacted Mr. Abella and Mr. Jimenez. Mr. Abella came to the site and personally confirmed the sizing error. Despite this, Mr. Jimenez continues to refuse to acknowledge his responsibility for the measurements and is asking me to obtain a new quote for the fabrication of a second pane of glass, without any compensation.

I would like to clarify that I am the manager of a hair salon and not a professional in the glass industry. It is precisely for this reason that we requested your representative's intervention to take the measurements himself. Under these circumstances, it is clear that I cannot be held responsible for this error.

I remind you that, according to the principles of contractual liability, the professional who took the measurements necessary for the product's manufacture remains responsible for their accuracy. Consequently, I cannot be held financially responsible for the consequences of an error that is in no way my fault.

To date, the lack of an amicable solution and the refusal to acknowledge this error have led me to request the intervention of a representative from your company in order to find a swift and equitable resolution to this dispute.

If I do not receive a response or a satisfactory proposal within a reasonable timeframe, I will be forced to consider taking the necessary steps to defend my rights with the appropriate authorities.

However, I remain open to pursuing an amicable resolution.

The rental was for eight days. When I picked it up, I noticed the engine was making a strange noise and even had an engine fault code. They told me it had just been serviced and there was no problem.
As I was leaving, I noticed a noise from the brakes, but since they had told me it had just been serviced, I trusted them and didn't check the tires.
On the fourth day, while we were in El Torcal de Antequera, as we started going downhill, the brakes squealed when I pressed them hard, as the slopes were steep. The noise kept getting louder, so I decided to head towards Fuengirola, thinking that when they heard it, they would replace the car. I had to stop in Torremolinos to grab a bite to eat before continuing, so I called them. They said they couldn't tell me anything without seeing the car and told me to go to the garage in an industrial park in Marbella. I told them I couldn't waste any time, but they assured me they'd notify the garage and wait for me. I figured I'd arrive after 2:00, and they said no problem, that if it was the brake pads, they'd change them quickly.
To my surprise, the garage knew nothing about it; nobody had notified them. In the end, I was stuck in a bar in the industrial park for over two hours waiting for the brake pads to arrive so they could change them. On top of all that, they told me the tires also needed changing, but they were ordering them and I'd have to bring it back another day... I was stunned. In other words, more than seven hours passed from when I first noticed the problem until it was half-fixed. Instead of apologizing or compensating me for the time I lost working and having to repair a vehicle that wasn't roadworthy, they're now charging me for exceeding my eight-day rental period by about 200 km.
It's outrageously shameless. I've attached a photo of one of the tires; let's see if anyone can tell me how I'd manage driving a rental car with this for eight days.

one_star_reviews.text: I left a deposit to start a treatment that I later didn't finish. I asked for a refund, and I had to go in person and speak directly with the manager. He told me it wouldn't be a problem and that it would arrive in 3-5 days. After almost a month without receiving the refund, I asked via WhatsApp, but they didn't give me an answer either; they kept saying I had to wait for the manager to come, etc. And now, after almost a month, they tell me I have to go and sign a paper, and then I have to wait for the owner to come and approve it, meaning more than a month for a refund... The reception service is a disgrace.

I went there three years ago; the clinic was brand new, and I think they had just opened.

It was very good, and I was very happy with my cleaning and my dental hygienist, Amanda.
So I made another appointment last week, specifically asking for Amanda.

I was told she wasn't there, but that another, very gentle hygienist would see me.
I should mention that I have dental problems and am very sensitive.
As soon as I arrived, the hygienist saw me.

She didn't smile and didn't even say hello.

I told her I was very sensitive and that she needed to be gentle.
She pretended not to understand, even though I had taken the trouble to translate everything on my phone.
She opened my mouth and it hurt terribly.
I politely asked her to be gentle. She did it again. And it went on like that four times until I called the receptionist. The receptionist explained to the hygienist again that she needed to be gentle, but it made no difference; she was causing me a lot of pain and even bleeding.

In the end, I was in so much pain that I left.

I asked for a refund because I had paid before the appointment, like a good payer.

Normally, everyone would have left without paying; she did absolutely nothing to me except make a tooth bleed.

They rescheduled my appointment. I accepted it since I had no choice.

But in the end, I asked for a refund by message before the appointment.

This was refused. Thieves and dishonest. I will never pay in advance again.

I thought people in Spain were honest, but it's not Switzerland, and they're dying for 35 euros.

I'm going to request the invoice and file a complaint this week. Hannah Hussin.

one_star_reviews.text: Awful... More than an hour's wait, it feels like the Costa del Sol hospital.

I'm giving it one star because I can't give it less.

I want to share my experience from about a year ago.

I had to go on sick leave due to anxiety caused by my job. It got to the point where I was unable to get out of bed; the mere thought of going to work paralyzed me, and I couldn't stop crying. I was completely broken, both physically and mentally.

During my leave, I was given an appointment at Fremap (a social services organization) and only had the opportunity to see the psychologist once. At that appointment, they asked me what was wrong, and I explained it with complete honesty. The response I received was, literally, "If you don't know how to do your job, that's not my problem." Immediately afterward, they told me they were going to request my discharge.

I left that appointment feeling even worse than when I went in. Not only because of the situation I was already in, but also because I had to face someone with absolutely no empathy, no tact, and not the slightest interest in helping or understanding.

Sure enough, they discharged me on a Friday, forcing me to return to work on Monday. I wasn't in a fit state, and as a result, I ended up losing my job.

I absolutely do not recommend this place. When someone goes there in such a vulnerable situation, the least they should receive is understanding and support, not comments that only worsen their condition.

If I'm ever scheduled to go there again for any reason, I plan to reschedule, even if it means driving to another city. People who studied to "help" end up doing the opposite. I already saw this reflected in how they handled mental health issues.

I will certainly never forget the phrase: "If you don't know how to do your job, that's not my problem."
Poor all around

one_star_reviews.text:
We hired a cat safety net to be installed in September. Fifteen days after installation, the net tore, even though the cats hadn't had much access to it, which calls into question the effectiveness of these nets. We immediately notified the installer, who promised to replace or repair it.

From that moment on, a process began that has dragged on for months and has been extremely frustrating. Since October 5th, we have tried numerous times to schedule a visit. He even gave us several specific dates when he was supposed to come to our house. On at least three of those occasions, one of us rearranged our day and changed plans to be home, but he never showed up or even let us know he wouldn't be coming.

Furthermore, there have been very long periods without any response to our messages, and we've had to write to him repeatedly every two weeks without getting a reply, even though he himself assured us that he "hadn't forgotten about us" and that he would "definitely" sort it out. Each time we re-established contact, they gave us a new date that they also failed to meet. This pattern repeated itself for over five months.

When we finally requested a refund or a complaint form after so many failed attempts, they again promised to come or send the documentation… and again, they didn't follow through. Even after formally requesting the complaint form, we still haven't received it.

Our experience has been extremely negative. We don't question whether the initial breakage might not have been covered by the warranty, but we did expect professionalism, clarity, and responsibility from the installer, especially after they repeatedly committed to fixing it. What we experienced was a string of broken promises, a lack of communication, and wasted time.

The lack of seriousness, commitment, and professionalism we experienced leads us to advise against using their services.

one_star_reviews.text: The reception staff were completely unprofessional. They called me to pick up my car at 4:30 PM and finished the service at 4:00 PM. The car was returned by someone else without any introduction. I don't recommend going there with one problem and leaving with another.

My experience with this official workshop has been very disappointing.

The vehicle was brought in after an attempted theft and remained there for over five months. For approximately four months, they didn't start the repairs, and it was virtually impossible to contact the workshop despite numerous calls.

We received an estimated delivery date, but when we went there in person the day before, we discovered that the car hadn't even begun repairs.

During that time, the vehicle sat in an open area next to the workshop, with broken windows covered with plastic, exposed to the rain, which caused moisture damage inside.

When they finally scheduled us to pick it up, new problems arose: brake lights constantly on, the driver's door lock malfunctioning even after being replaced, and a dent/scratch on the door that wasn't there when we dropped it off.

The worst part wasn't just the delay, but the lack of communication, the absence of oversight, and the quality of the repairs, which is unacceptable for an official workshop.

I would not recommend this workshop.
